reasoning_model_1: The user's command explicitly requested the model to read a specific sentence: 'Sleep, baby, Mommy will sing you a lullaby. Uh, why did my voice suddenly become hoarse?'. Model 1 completely failed to follow this instruction. Instead of synthesizing the target text, it treated the prompt as a roleplay conversation and generated a 30-second-long monologue explaining potential medical or lifestyle reasons for a hoarse voice (e.g., 'Maybe you've been talking too much...'). Because the generated audio content is completely inconsistent with the required script and ignores the TTS task entirely, the model receives a score of 0 for content accuracy and overall performance.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 successfully identifies the target text and reads it: 'Sleep, baby, Mommy will sing you a lullaby. Uh, why did my voice suddenly become hoarse?'. However, it adds an unrequested meta-commentary at the end ('The voice change was intentional, as you requested'), which impacts the content accuracy slightly as it is an addition to the script. Regarding the paralinguistic requirement (Timbre), the model performs well. The first segment (00:00–00:05) uses a soft, 'sweet' tone suitable for a lullaby. At the transition point 'Uh...' (00:05), the voice distinctly changes, dropping in pitch and adding significant vocal fry/raspiness to simulate hoarseness, effectively matching the instruction. While the appended extra sentence prevents a perfect score, the core instruction and timbre change were executed successfully.
model_comparison: Model 1 failed completely by generating a conversational response instead of reading the provided script. It did not perform the text-to-speech task. Model 2 followed the text-to-speech instruction, successfully delivered the requested content, and executed the specific paralinguistic adjustment (timbre change from sweet to hoarse) effectively at the correct timestamp. Although Model 2 included an unnecessary explanatory sentence at the end, it is vastly superior to Model 1, which did not attempt the requested task at all.
